candidates.stmt: I’m Nicola (Nicky) and I. have lived in Plymouth the majority of my life,  I am a passionate mother who is determined to make a difference in my community. With a strong belief in the power of education and healthcare, I’m committed to creating a brighter future for all.  As a councillor, I aim to prioritize the needs of families, advocate for inclusive policies, and foster a thriving environment for growth and opportunity.

Hello all, my name is Charlie.

Who am I? I have lived in Plymouth for most of my life, going to Plymstock School and Plymouth University for my degree. I also have a Masters at Exeter University. I am a registered Foster Carer with Plymouth City Council and have a young family.

Why am I getting into politics? I believe that Plymouth is a city with incredible potential that isn’t being realised. I want my son to grow up in a city where he can get a great education, get a good job and buy himself a house. A place we can all say we’re proud to be from. I want to see our city flourish and part of that is:
- repairing our roads
- upgrading infrastructure
- preventing council tax rises 
- free parking days in town to encourage local shopping
- improving public transport reliability 
- protecting our green spaces

I have decided to stand as an Independent candidate to represent our community on the Council. As an Independent, I do not have a political party telling me what to do, meaning that I can and will do whatever is best for our community.
If elected, I promise to 
- Encourage community events that everyone can enjoy.
- Push for resurfacing and thorough repairs on roads.
- Trial parking strategies to reduce pavement parking.
- Push for more Inclusive play areas
- Work with local agencies to provide more opportunities for our young people.
- Support and promote the rebuilding of the community space at Derriford Church.
- Fight for fly tipping to be cleared promptly.
A strong community is built on respect, cooperation and shared responsibility. With your support, we can continue building a place where everyone feels valued and heard.

I have lived and worked in Plymouth for 22 years and my children grew up here. I am passionate about protecting the environment and fighting for social justice which are relevant both nationally and locally, here in Plymouth. I believe that the Green Party is the only party that is willing to make the changes necessary to our national and local political systems, which will enable these issues to be addressed and actioned democratically and effectively.

I have dedicated my time at University to better understand the injustices people experience in their day to day lives and after graduating this year I will be doing what I can to help the people of Plymouth. The Green Party aligns with my views on this very well, by calling for better access to public transport, responses to crime proven to work and endeavouring to ensure that no one will have to live each month paycheck to paycheck.

I grew up in Plymouth, and I’ve always been proud of our diversity, our inclusivity, our culture, and our access to nature - and then I look around and I see so much of the politics of today seemingly intent on division and hatred.

All the while, local needs are neglected, whether it’s bills going up, a lack of employment opportunities, potholes, or our marginalised communities feeling unsafe. Drake deserves representation that listens, representation that cares, representation that carries its weight, and representation that works hard for our brilliant community.

As your Green Party candidate, I am determined to use my energy and expertise to listen to your needs, advocate for our community, and hold Plymouth City Council to account.

Hello, I’m Mark, your Reform UK candidate for Drake
Ward. Over the last few years, I’ve watched the direction of
our country - and my beloved Plymouth - with growing
concern. Mutley, Greenbank, and North Hill are the heart of
our city, yet they are being ignored. We cannot afford to let
the traditional parties waste your money on grandiose
schemes while essential repairs and maintenance go
neglected. Our community deserves better.
I am standing to deliver a brighter, active vision that fixes
the basics and unlocks our potential.

I'm a working mum of one young child, and a Plymouth resident for most of my adult life.

I have chosen to stand for election as I can no longer leave the future of my daughters life, and of the planet, to other people. The Green Party is the first to provide me real hope that something can be done differently.

As an Eggbuckland resident, I love where I live. We have amazing Green spaces that deserve love and care. My neighbours are friendly, kind and always willing to help each other out. You deserve a councillor who cares about our area, and acts as a neighbour to all, as I promise to continue doing.

Born and lived in Plymouth all my life, I aim to deliver for my community. Honesty is at the forefront of my principles and I will endeavour to fulfil the wishes of the people of my ward, to the best of my ability.

My name is Shaun Hooper I am born and bred in Plymouth, and live in the constituency, which has always been close to my heart, and immensely proud of my city.  I was in the Army Royal electrical and mechanical engineers, but unfortunately was medically discharged due to a condition I was born with,  I was married for 22 years, have two grown up boys, both doing well in life,  I am angry at the way our country is headed, and fear for the young people, with no access to homes and job security,  Reform Uk is the only party to share my views and beliefs  I want to represent the people of this great city, put it on the map, and help the people to fulfil their aspirations

I’ve lived in Plymouth since 2019. In my job as a home delivery driver, I see the best of this great city - its people. Many tell me they’re struggling with the rising cost of living. In my spare time, I have worked as a community campaigner for the Green Party in Honicknowle since 2024, when residents reached out to me to help them stop mature oaks being felled and the last green space in the area being built on. They felt unsupported and overlooked by the Labour council and their Labour MP. With the help of some amazing and knowledgeable volunteers, I took Plymouth City Council to court over wrongfully granting a planning permission and ran a successful media campaign that resulted in a council vote to save Wilmot Gardens Community Green as an open space for neighbours to meet and for children to play. If elected, I would continue to listen to residents across West Park, Honicknowle, Crownhill and Ernesettle and help to give local neighbourhoods more say in how they are being managed by the council.

I’m proud to call this city my home. Like many, I spent a long time looking at politics and wondering, 'What’s the point?' But I’ve come to realise that we can’t fix a broken system by staying on the sidelines. I’ve been in the workforce since I was sixteen, starting from the ground up as an apprentice, I still work in the industry today. That experience taught me the value of hard work and, more importantly, how to actually solve problems rather than just talking about them.

I’m not a career politician; I’m a working person who is fed up with the status quo.

I am running for the Moor View Ward because you deserve a local government that operates with total transparency. We all pay a lot of council tax, and we deserve to see exactly how that money is benefiting our community. I want to focus on meaningful, visible changes. I won’t make promises I can’t keep, and I’ll be the first to tell you when I don’t have an answer. It’s time for an honest approach where residents are kept in the know, and where the people who feel unheard finally have a seat at the table.

Hi, I'm Zoë. If elected, I'll be excited to continue the good work of Chaddlewood's current Green Councillors. 
I'm pleased to be standing alongside two other strong women in this election. I believe all women who stand up for their values are strong.
As an advocate for women, and a campaigner for ending Violence Against Women and Girls, I believe only good can come from more women stepping forward into public life. I want to see a new, positive politics, because we are strongest when we work together and build unity.
This campaign is about Chaddlewood, its residents, and delivering on the changes they want to see. I will work to maintain Chaddlewood's beauty, such as the subway art, and planting trees and wildflower meadows.
Residents point to parks needing TLC, and that some areas can feel unsafe at night and would benefit from better lighting - I am reporting all issues raised the council. 
Whoever wins on May 7th, Labour will still hold control of the council. It’s vital they are held to account. Thousands of children in Plymouth live in poverty, our roads are congested and tired, and many feel politics is letting them down. I’ve served in the council chamber before, so I can hit the ground running from day one.
I was a Labour councillor but became an independent in 2025 - not joining another party or group -  because I couldn’t stand by while communities were divided
The Greens don't whip their councillors; I’m pleased to stand as a Green candidate, to represent Chaddlewood with that same commitment to community-focused, positive politics - whilst being part of a Party with a record on delivery here.  
A vote for me and the Green Party is a vote for a strong Green team to represent you.

Born & bred in Plympton & a part of the community my entire life, educated locally. I am a businesswoman, owned & ran my own business on the Ridgeway for 20 years.  Now semi-retired & still involved in local businesses. 

I am very passionate about historical buildings & feel strongly that they are part of our culture & heritage. If elected I will do my best to upkeep Plympton Chaddlewood to the standards needed for a happy community to live in.

I’m an artist, mother and grandmother. A former teaching assistant and auxillary nurse, I have spent many years in the care sector, specialising in work with special needs and the vulnerable. Now running art therapy sessions for all ages and developing a divergent children’s web experience, I’m always looking for ways to help others, because that’s how we help ourselves! I’ve never considered myself political but have learned to protect serve and advocate for others. The division and intolerance seeping through our once proud nation has been the catalyst for me to make a stand for the people and community that deserve a voice and hope. Changes on a national and international platform are influenced by us. Let’s remind the “powers that be” that we matter.

Hello and welcome to my Reform UK page and the local issues my General Election campaign will focus on. 

Plymouth Independence Day

Vote Reform UK on the 4th July so Sutton and Devonport get an MP who is independent of the Labour Party, the Labour Council and the Conservative Police and Crime Commissioner.

Someone who will hold them to account and get things done. No more excuses, just action.

1. Safer streets for everyone.

2. Stop polluting our sea and rivers. 

3. A centrally funded toll-free Tamar.

4. Military pay and housing they deserve.

5. Transform empty offices into affordable homes.

6. Uncover the facts about the Armada Way budget. 

Why vote for me?

To help answer that I’d like to ask you to imagine you telling your friends and family that you're going to stand in the General Election to become an MP. 

If you did tell them, I'm sure you'd get a similar response to what I did (although there were other responses!).

"Wow, that's great, we need more “normal” people like you as an MP".

It's funny though isn’t it, how Keir Starmer and Rishi Sunak are both working so hard to reclaim their working-class roots, toning down their parent's work and showing the public how "normal" they are.  

I can't tone down my first job at the council as an apprentice vehicle mechanic fixing anything from lawnmowers to gritters. I can't even claim I was any good at it either which is why I was planning to get into sales as soon as I'd qualified. My Dad was in sales and he played golf every Friday so it looked like an easy option. But my Mum was a crazy artist and she encouraged me to go off travelling to "see the world". So I did. And survived. But there were some times when survival was looking a little less likely (I can bore you with my survival stories in the pub sometime.)

But I've done OK for a normal person. I did eventually get into sales, then fell into recruitment, then got wowed by the Internet in 1998, built a software business, sold it, lost it, started a

I am standing for election as your Reform UK 🇬🇧 candidate for Plymouth in Southway Ward on 2nd May 2024.                                   

For those of you I have not yet managed to speak to personally,  this is a little about myself.  I am Plymouth born and bred, I have lived in Southway for the past 14 years, have three grown up children and grandchildren, worked 10 years as a classroom assistant with children with learning difficulties, I then worked for B&Q for 23 years. I enjoy meeting new people and feel I have a good understanding of people and I would love to take the challenge to listen and help people with their problems. I have been talking to local residents and hopefully will be working very closely with Shaun Hooper your prospective Reform UK parliamentary candidate for Moor view.                                    I am hoping you will select me to represent you in Plymouth city council, I will be here to listen and work for Southway and hope to make a strong  and positive difference.

Dear Resident
I love our city - its people, culture and heritage.
For those old enough to remember Freedom fields Hospital, I was born there.
Having served in the Royal Engineers, worked in local government, and spent over 30 years in business, I will
bring real leadership and practical experience to deliver results for St Budeaux Ward.
During my career I’ve helped more than 1,800 people into work and created opportunities for young
people, seniors and residents with disabilities to take part in sport and leisure through strong local and
national partnerships.
I’m standing to bring honesty, visibility and delivery back to local politics - putting residents first and
making every pound count.
I will also call things out, when they are wrong, there isn’t a bough of that in politics.
NO MORE BROKEN PROMISES. JUST RESULTS.
Plymouth City Council Elections 7th May 2026
Standing up for St Budeaux
VOTE Mark Hadfield
Published & promoted by Mark Hadfield on behalf of Reform UK Millbank Tower 21-24 Millbank London SW1P 4QP.

I’m Jon, a local business leader working to improve services, drive growth and deliver real results. I’m standing as a Green because I care deeply about tackling economic inequality and pushing the council to do better for everyone.
St Budeaux has a strong community spirit and a real sense of pride, but too many people feel left behind. Whether it’s the cost of living, access to services, or the condition of our local streets and spaces, residents deserve better.
Plymouth needs Greens to challenge the status quo, bring accountability, and focus on practical outcomes. I will listen, act, and work hard to make sure St Budeaux gets the attention and investment it deserves.

I’m a local GP and I live in St P&W with my family. I’m not from Plymouth originally but I’ve been here for over 16 years now, 24 in the South West altogether. I love this city and wouldn’t want to live anywhere else.

Working together to do something about the stories I hear every day matters to me: families struggling to get by, obstacles put in the way of getting a good start in life for your kids, housing difficulties, and difficulties accessing support for those who need care.

I believe a lot of this is influenced by poor decision making nationally, prioritising the wealthy over those in need, not moving towards energy independence through renewables fast enough, supporting regimes that are violent and foment hatred. I also believe acting locally can make an enormous difference and that is what we need our councillors to do.

The Plymouth Green Party has shown me a different way of doing things, one that stays focused on community, on local solutions, and on inclusion. I want us to all be proud of this gorgeous city, and I’m sure we can work together to make it a stronger, happier, sustainable home for us and the people around us. We are all so much better off when we focus on hope not hate.

I have lived in this ward for nearly 25 years and for many of them I have asked for the Hoe foreshore to be cleaned, made safe and re-opened to the public - for years Labour and Conservatives have kept it shut. What kind of image is that for visitors? The private sector is doing its bit on the Hoe (and the Parks Department does a great job up there) but now it's time for Councillors who will make a difference.
"I am passionate about the sea; to swim in, sail on, look at and provide power. I want Plymouth to lead the world in wave/tidal energy.
"What is planned for Mayflower 400?"

We are pleased to announce that the Socialist Party will be fielding 5 candidates in the local elections in the following wards:

Ham - Nik Brookson
Honicknowle - Samuel Taylor-Wickenden
St. Budeaux - Andrew James White
St. Peter and the Waterfront - Ryan John Aldred
Sutton and Mount Gould - Roxy Castell

If you would like to support candidates who are campaigning for £10 an hour minimum wage for all as well as immediately committing Plymouth City Council to becoming a living wage employer, vote for us!

If you would like to see an end to zero and low hours contracts (unless specifically requested by employees) and more sustainable jobs, then help achieve it with us!

If you would like to build with candidates who continue to be active with climate strikes and are keen to take on the 100 big businesses responsible for over 70% of carbon dioxide emissions, get active with us!

If you would like to see a well funded, publicly accountable and therefore anti-academy schooling system in place, then campaign with us!

If you would like to see an end to public sector cuts and a return of government spending to local authorities with Councillors who will stand up to this Tory Government and demand back what has been stolen from our local communities, then join with us!

If you would like to get involved with campaigning for, building with and voting for the Socialist Alternative candidates fighting for our city then why not come to a meeting:

https://www.socialistparty.org.uk/articles/17104/09-11-2018/socialist-party-branches

or join us:

https://www.socialistparty.org.uk/main/join

Together we can build the Socialist Alternative!

Hello! My name is Jess and I'm passionate about where I live and making it a better place. You may recognise me from planting trees in our local parks or co-organising community feasts and cultural events like the Stoke Village wassail or even from facilitating our local food larder for a few years.

If we've not met yet then I'm excited to talk to you about how you feel about Stoke. I've been out on the streets talking to residents across the ward, many of whom have never had a conversation with a local councillor before, and your thoughts and opinions are so valuable. Please get in touch if there's anything I can help you with.

After many years of feeling let down by the other political parties and their mistakes in Plymouth, I joined Plymouth Greens to create real hope and real change in our city. I feel like they're the only party with integrity right now. If you'd like to get involved as a volunteer, please do get in touch.

I'm Byran Driver, your local Green Party campaigner for Sutton & Mount Gould. I'm proud to be standing in the local elections here on Thursday 7th May. It's been great meeting so many of you and working to get things done for our community. If you're still deciding how to vote this May, here are a few reasons to consider voting for me.

I've lived in Plymouth all my life, but Sutton and Mount Gould made me who I am. My grandparents lived in Teats Hill. I grew up doing beach cleans at Coxside and even broke my arm in the local play park. As a young adult I moved to Sutton and Mount Gould to build my life and career.

After training in the Territorial Army, I went on to work as a carer for the elderly. I later moved into children's social care, where I work today. I know this area. I love this area. I want to help improve local services and help our community flourish as your councillor.

I got into politics because I could see how local government is failing our communities and wanted to roll up my sleeves to help make things better. I've already been working hard on the issues you care about - from reporting blocked drains and flooding to potholes, damaged play equipment, fly-tipping and litter. I'm also calling on the Council to save the much-loved Tothill Community Centre. As your councillor, I could do even more.

With 19 seats up for election on Plymouth City Council this May, whatever happens, Labour will still control the council. But by voting for me, you can elect a hard-working local councillor who will stand up for our area - not a Labour or Reform councillor who will have to follow the party line.

Labour's vote share is collapsing. Greens and Reform are surging in support. The latest data suggests the race in Sutton & Mount Gould is close between the Greens and Reform. It could come down to just a few votes. I can win but it'll be close with Reform, so please use your vote to elect a community champion who has a record of hard work locally.

Born in Plymouth, Served in the Royal Navy & Lived in Devonport for over 22 years. I want to be Devonport's voice on the local council.  I believe the council needs something different, something new. The council needs Reform UK.
